The iPhone 15 is the most confusing phone I've used I spent the last 2 weeks with it and it definitely has features that rival even the pro and Ultra phones like how usable it is under bright sunlight and how shockingly good the main camera is just look at these two photos here can you even tell which one's taken by the pro iPhone however what makes it confusing is that the iPhone 15 is still missing some very common features that I even remember my S21 having nearly 3 years ago and this can certainly make it less competitive than other phones in its $800 price category but definitely not in the physical aspect this new matte glass really makes the foam feel different this is probably my favorite texture of a matte glass it feels almost more soft and smooth than the 15 Pro and the Samsung's and this frosted look is pretty cool some people may think that the colors this year are very washed out and I don't know about the other colors but this shade of green looks really good I think it has just the right amount of saturation and it feels very fresh this color and material are also very good at resisting fingerprints and same with these matte aluminum SES I also like that the corners remain rounded and the edges have become more rounded it makes holding this foam more comfortable in any orientation overall the iPhone 15 feels really nice in the hand and I also personally like how light it is but a more unique aspect is a two-tone design the aluminum size the camera bump and Apple logo are all a darker shade of green compared to the Frosted back and if you pay attention you'll see even the new USBC Port is green on the inside which is a crazy attention to detail so it's a shame that the transfer speed is still USB 2.0 which isn't any faster than the old lightning Port now the faster USB 3-speed isn't as badly needed on this phone compared to the pro iPhones because it can't take the larger Pro reses and raw files anyways but if you ever want to transfer lots of photos then the USB 2.0 speed will definitely haunt you and it's not like a USB 3.0 Port would be too expensive to add to this phone it's an $800 phone and it really should have nicer features like an actually fastport not to mention that many phones at much lower price points still have a US USB 3.0 Port but this is just the first of many instances where the iPhone 15 feels intentionally held back other than that the USBC does provide some benefits over lightning and giving the iPhone more functions and accessories such as this USBC SSD and now you can also charge some accessories with it so USBC can be a pretty big change for some but for me after a year with the 14 pro I rarely even plugged it in with a cable because Max saave is so good it will remain my favorite way to charge especially because the USBC Port doesn't even allowed to charge any faster than before and Max safe accessories like a charge stand portable battery car mount are also very clean to use with minimal cables involved now going to the display it certainly has very impressive specs it's super sharp at 461 pixels per inch which is the same as on the iPhone 15 Pro and now it can go just as bright as well to 2,000 nits Peak brightness which is slightly more than the s23 is 1750 nits Peak brightness and it's a significant Improvement to last gen's 1200 nits this upgrade definitely makes it a lot more visible under bright sunlight the screen looks great but once you interact with it you'll realize that it's still 60 HZ and this is definitely an anomaly when you look at its competitors no comparably priced Android phones runs at 60 HZ even a $300 phone the Samsung a23 is 90 HZ coming from using 120 HZ on The Daily I can definitely notice a difference the iPhone 15's animations just feel noticeably less smooth like opening and closing up an app scrolling through something changing between the cameras limiting to 60 HZ is one of the ways that apple is using to differentiate from the Pro Models but I think this move is really hurting the iPhone 15's appeal like even though it has the same processor as the iPhone 14 pro having less smooth of a screen just makes it feel not nearly as nice and unlike the pro iPhones the screen cannot change its refresh rate so despite having an OLED screen the iPhone 15 does not have always on display probably because refreshing at 60 HZ continuously would drain too much battery it really is a shame because I actually really like the always on display having a dim wallpaper makes it very aesthetically pleasing and the lock screen widgets that also show up gives good info just at a glance such as these weather widgets from overdrop not having variable refresh rate screen also affects the standby mode this is like a cool screen saver for the iPhone when charging and horizontal it can show all sorts of widgets some photos and many clock designs I really like this feature on the pro iPhones where it's shown all the time however on the iPhone 15 it's a lot less useful because it turns off after less than a minute and I have to tap it again to wake it but I've also noticed it randomly turns on sometimes I find the flashing on and off pretty distracting so I've actually disabled this feature and one more thing that feels missing on the iPhone 15 is a telephoto camera I'm reminded of this every time I try to zoom in this year the main camera is higher resolution and it can give you pretty decent photos at two time zoom but at three time zoom even next to the Samsung S21 my almost 3-year-old Android phone it's noticeably not as good and it only gets worse the more you zoom in but that aside I've been super super impressed with how good the main camera is it's now 48 megapixels which matches the iPhone 15 PR's main camera they're not exactly the same the sensor on the 15 is slightly smaller But the lens is faster and it lets them more light and the result is that it looks almost the same as the iPhone 15 Pro Max's photo even when zoomed in a ton both of these images are 24 megapixels which is the new default on all the new iPhones this is a really great change since the higher resolution makes the image more detailed with less sharpening artifacts and it's only about 1.5 times larger than 12 megap photos before this phone the best camera in the price range is by far the s23 with a really nice 50 megap main camera and a good telephoto camera too however now the iPhone 15's main camera is notably better than the s23 is just take a look at these two photos both taken using their default modes the iPhone 15 photo has so much more detail in the tree and the building it's the same when indoors too the Samsung Photo has a lot more sharpening and looks less natural than the iPhone's photo but actually this difference is partially due to the iph iPhone photo being 24 megapixels versus 12 megapix on the Samsung when they're both at their highest resolution 48 and 50 megapixels the difference in quality is a lot smaller although the tree and the building still look a little bit better and more detailed in the iPhone's photo it's really great that the iPhone 15's main camera image quality is on par with the best out there but not just that there's actually a big hidden Advantage because it can focus as close as 4 and 1/2 in whereas the 15 Pro Max can only focus as close as about 6 and 1/2 in and any closer it will switch the lower quality macro mode so for the Times where you're trying to take a photo of something close up the iPhone 15 will probably be better for it but do note that the iPhone 15 Pro Max can still Focus much closer with its macro mode although the image quality is much lower than with the main camera the iPhone 15 doesn't have this mode which also means that its Ultra wide lens cannot change its focus for environmental shots this is perfectly fine however when there's a subject involved being able to focus like how the Pro Models can is still an advantage but but to be fair at the same price range the s23 is ultra wide also cannot change its focus and their quality is pretty similar but the Samsung image is quite a bit more sharpened as you can see in these leaves here for the selfie I think the iPhone can be a bit too aggressive with the processing like sharpening and contrast which tends to not produce the most pleasing skin here it is next to the s23 which still has my favorite selfie camera for video taken on the main camera the quality is actually shockingly similar between the iPhone 15 and the s23 and the stabilization is pretty similar too this year the iPhone 15 gained the ability to add portrait mode effect to photos and post and it can do it on pretty much any subject this is pretty cool and I can see myself using it occasionally another software feature that I like is a New Horizon level overall the iPhone 15's soul to cameras are great and to be honest I'm shocked that the main camera photos are just as good as from the pro model but unlike most of its competitors not having a telephoto camera puts it at a distinct disadvantage when taking photos of anything that's even slightly farther away so it might have the best main camera in its price range but definitely not the best camera system and as for the software so the notch is replaced by the dynamic Island and after using it on the iPhone 14 pro throughout the past year I can say that it is very helpful in day-to-day use for being able to monitor something while still doing other things on the phone and there are well-known apps that have a function for this like uber shows ride information and United Airlines shows flight status there but you can also just have a cute little pixel pet on the island and there's also this funny and honestly addicting game called Hit the island the rest of the software also got a refresh with iOS 17 I've been on it for a while now and here are some of my favorite features the first one is a lock screen I love being able to make multiple lock screens each with their own widgets and in iio 17 there are some new astronomy wallpapers and live photo wallpapers are brought back as well now when the phone wakes the live photo plays in slow motion which is really funny and it has become one of my favorite wallpaper types widgets on the lock screen screen are very handy but now they can actually be interactive like I can check things off of my to-do list right from the lock screen which leads me to the home screen that now also supports interactive widgets other than that though the home screen is just the same as before I think some theming support like what's available on Android phones can be nice so that there's a quick way to make the home screen look nicer okay and this next one is honestly an underrated feature and the Photo Gallery live photos can be changed to have a bunch of different effects like the bounce or long exposure which can look very cool for waterfalls and things like that and social media apps like Instagram and iPhone can do all sorts of animations for live photo like the slow-mo effect there are also some pretty entertaining features like making stickers in the photo gallery and effects in FaceTime like these hearts these are not really useful things and some people probably won't ever use them but I think they can make calling or texting family and friends a lot more fun so I think it's great I also wish that the new action button on the iPhone 15 Pros can be given to the 15 as well having a shortcut button is pretty handy probably next year the action button will be on all the new iPhones because there's this trend where the Bas iPhone will get features from the pro model one year later and speaking of which the iPhone 15 got the chip that was in the iPhone 14 pro for the CPU it's quite a bit faster than the s23 Snapdragon H Gen 2 chip for both single core and multi-core you rarely feel CPU difference in real life but being faster can still help it stay smooth for a longer time however for GPU the a16 is about 19% slower than the hn2 also the iPhone 15 thermal throttles almost instantly after just 2 minutes the performance significantly decreases whereas the s23 is performance just slowly declines so the iPhone 15 is definitely not the best for more demanding games but even for lighter games the 60 HZ screen alone already makes it not great for everyday use though like web browsing and watching some videos the performance isn't a concern at all also in a way the thermo throttling helps to preserve the battery life after 40 minutes of running a gaming Benchmark the iPhone dropped 22% while the s23 dropped 30% but when they're not doing something intensive the battery percentage drop is actually very similar after playing a 1080p live stream for 6 and 1/2 hours while on Wi-Fi with airplane mode and around 70% brightness the iPhone dropped 44% and the s23 dropped 47% now the iPhone does have a 14% smaller battery at justes 3,349 milliamp hourss so it is quite a bit more efficient than the s23 neither of these two phones have spectacular battery life since they are small phones with a relatively small battery I found that with the iPhone 15 as long as I'm not doing anything too crazy it can last all day without a problem okay so the iPhone 15 is really a conundrum while it has impeccable design an impressive main camera and decent battery life it does fall short in certain areas like the screen refresh rate absence of the telephoto lens and outdated Port speed these intentional emissions compared to both its Pro counterpart and even older less expensive competitors makes it a challenging sell in its price category for those who are into the Apple ecosystem and maybe primarily valuing the design and Main camera quality the iPhone 15 can be great but if you want more than that then there are much better options out there at the same price if you found this review helpful please remember to like And subscribe you can follow me on my other socials and you can watch more here

Hey everyone.So, the iPhone 17 series just launched a few weeks back and the hype is real.All the new features, the new designs, it's exciting stuff, but all that new tech got me thinking.What about the phones that are now a couple of years old?Specifically, this one, the iPhone 15.It launched back in 2023, and now in late 2025 with two new generations out, you can find it on the secondary market for around $450 to $500.And that price, well, that changes the conversation completely.So, the big question is, is it still worth it?Should you buy one or maybe upgrade to one from an older phone?Let's talk about it.In this video, we're going to cover everything from the design and display to how the performance holds up with the brand new iOS 26.We look at the camera, the battery life, and of course, talk about the things it doesn't have like Apple Intelligence.Just for full disclosure, this blue iPhone 15 has been my secondary backup phone for about a year now.I first started using it back in October 2024.It's not my daily driver.It's more of a backup, a device I use for testing new iOS betas for some gaming or just when my main phone is on the charger.As of today, the battery health is still at a perfect 100% with 207 charge cycles, which is honestly pretty impressive, and we'll get into why a little later.Also, a quick note, everything I'm about to say about this phone pretty much applies to the iPhone 15 Plus as well.The only real differences are the bigger 6.7 in screen and the even better battery life.So, if you're someone who just wants a larger display or is a battery life absolutist, you can just mentally sub in the 15 plus here.I've seen a lot of positive comments on forums from people who own the Plus and they rave about its endurance.So, it's a really solid choice if you value that.So, what was so special about the iPhone 15 when it first came out?Looking back, I think this was the year the standard iPhone finally grew up.For a long time, the base models felt like they were a full generation behind the Pros, but the iPhone 15 really broke that pattern.It inherited three key pro features that make it age incredibly well.First, the design.Apple moved away from those sharp, flat edges of the iPhone 12, 13, and 14.This phone has softly contoured aluminum edges that just melt into the back glass.It feels so much more comfortable to hold, which was a common point of praise in long-term reviews back when it was new.And the back is this beautiful color infused matte textured glass.My blue one here still looks brand new with no fingerprints.It just feels premium in a way.Second, it got the dynamic island.This was a huge deal because it finally got rid of the notch on the standard model and unified the look of the entire iPhone lineup.It's not just for looks, though.I find it genuinely useful for things like timers, music controls, and live activities like tracking a food delivery without having to jump back into the app.It makes the phone feel modern and interactive, turning what used to be a dead space into something functional.And third, the big one, USBC.Finally, after years of the lightning port, having one cable to charge my iPhone, my iPad, and my Mac is just a massive quality of life improvement.I can even use this iPhone to charge up my AirPods or Apple Watch in a pinch with a USBC cable, which I've actually done a few times when I've been out and about.Now, it's true this is a USB 2.0 port, not the faster USB 3 you get on the Pro models.That means data transfer speeds are capped at 480 megabits per second, but let's be honest, for most people, this is a non-issue.I, like most users, transfer big files with AirDrop or just use iCloud.The main benefit here is the universal charging standard, not raw data speed.All, let's talk about the screen.It's a 6.1 in super retina XDR display, and it is beautiful.It's sharp, colors are fantastic, and it gets incredibly bright outdoors up to 2,000 nits peak brightness, which is still excellent even by 2025 standards.But we have to address this one.It's a 60 Hz panel.No promotion.On paper, this is the phone's biggest weakness.And if you're coming from a 120 Hz phone, you might notice it.But here's where the story gets interesting.This phone is running the latest iOS 26, which brought this massive redesign Apple is calling liquid glass.And I have to say, it's a gamecher.It's a significant leap from the flat design we saw starting with iOS 7, making something like iOS 18 feel, well, old now.Liquid Glass is all about these new translucent layered elements and incredibly fluid dynamic animations.It's just more expressive.And the performance of this new UI on the iPhone 15 is just buttery smooth.Apple's animation work here is topnotch.And honestly, it does a fantastic job of masking the 60 Hz refresh rate in day-to-day use.Swiping around the home screen, opening apps, it just feels fluid.When I first updated to iOS 26, the phone got a bit warm for the first day or so.That's pretty normal.The system is just reindexing all your files to settle in.But after that, it's been a great experience.I haven't noticed any performance dip, and battery life feels about the same as it did before.Powering all this is the A16 Bionic chip.This was the flagship chip from the iPhone 14 Pro, and it is still an absolute powerhouse.I ran a quick Geekbench 6 test on this phone, and I'm getting a single core score of 2,626 and a multi-core of 6,538.Those are still very respectable numbers for late 2025.Apps open instantly and general navigation is flawless.But the real key here, I think, is the 6 GB of RAM.When I review older base models like the iPhone 12 or 13, which have 4 GB of RAM, I constantly see apps reloading in the background.It's a real pain point as apps have gotten more demanding.With 6 GB on the iPhone 15, multitasking is so much better.Apps stay in memory longer, making the whole experience feel faster and more capable.For gaming, it's still very playable.High-end games run well.It can get a little warm during long sessions, but it's nothing out of the ordinary, and it never gets uncomfortable.Another huge upgrade that came from the Pro line was the main camera.For the first time on a base model, we got a 48 megapixel sensor.By default, it uses a process called pixel binning to create these incredibly detailed 24 megapixel photos.This gives you the best of both worlds.Amazing detail from the 48 megapixel sensor combined with the great light gathering capabilities of a 12 megapixel shot.All without creating massive file sizes in good light.The photos are just fantastic.The color science is natural and Smart HDR5 does a great job balancing bright skies and dark shadows.It's a reliable point andoot camera that consistently delivers great results.But here's the clever part.Because the sensor has so many pixels, Apple is able to do a high quality crop from the center to give you a two by telephoto zoom.And they're not wrong to call it optical quality.The results are sharp and detailed, much better than the mushy digital zoom you'd get on older iPhones.It effectively gives you a third lens, which is perfect for portraits or just getting a bit closer to your subject.And the video quality is still classic iPhone, which is to say it's excellent.You get 4K recording at up to 60 frames per second, cinematic mode for that blurry background effect, and a super stable action mode for when you're moving around a lot.For a phone at this price point, the video capabilities are absolutely top tier.Is it as versatile as the new iPhone 17?Of course not.But for the vast majority of people, this camera system is more than enough.It's a huge leap over something like an iPhone 11, 12, or 13, 14, and it holds its own surprisingly well, even in 2025.Okay, battery life as a backup phone, I'm not hammering it every single day.But on the days I do use it heavily for gaming, scrolling, and testing, it easily gets me through a full day, especially if I'm mostly on Wi-Fi, I'd say it's a solid all day phone for the average user.But here's something really important that Apple introduced with the iPhone 15 series.The batteries are designed to last longer.Apple officially states they're designed to retain 80% of their original capacity at 1,00 complete charge cycles.For comparison, older iPhones like the 14 and earlier were rated for just 500 cycles.This is a huge deal, especially when you're buying a used phone.It means a 2-year-old iPhone 15 likely has a much healthier battery than a 2-year-old iPhone 14 would.And my own experience backs this up.After a year and over 200 cycles, I'm still at 100% health.That's fantastic longevity.Now, let's be upfront about what this phone can't do.The iPhone 15 and 15 Plus do not support Apple Intelligence.You need an iPhone 15 Pro or newer for that.And for me, honestly, that's not a dealbreaker.Here's why.Apple Intelligence right now in late 2025 is still in its early stages.It's cool, but, it's like a beta for the heavyduty AI tasks that I actually do like research or writing assistance.I'm using more powerful dedicated tools anyway, like Google's Gemini or Chat GPT.These apps work perfectly fine on the iPhone 15.So, while it's a feature you're missing out on, I don't think it's a reason to avoid this phone, especially at this price.So, let's bring it all together.The iPhone 15 in late 2025 for $450 to $500, is it worth it?For me, the answer is a resounding yes.I think this is one of the best value smartphones you can buy right now.For that price, you're getting a package that is just incredibly strong.Let's break down who this phone is for.If you're coming from an older iPhone like the 11, 12, or even the 13, this will feel like a good upgrade.The jump in camera quality, the much brighter screen, the speed, the dynamic island, it's a night and day difference.If you're looking for an iPhone on a budget, I'd argue this is a much smarter buy than a brand new entry-level model.You're getting a more premium feel and a better core experience for your money.When you look at mid-range Android phones in this price range, they might offer a 120 Hz screen, but they often compromise on the processor, build quality, or camera processing to get there.This phone feels like a flagship, just a couple of years older.And if you're watching this and you already have an iPhone 15, keep it.As my experience with iOS 26 shows, this phone has so much life left in it.There is no compelling reason to spend the money on an upgrade unless you absolutely need Apple intelligence or a 120 Hz display.So, there you have it.The iPhone 15 in late 2025 is a modern classic.It's the phone where the standard model finally caught up in the ways that matter most.At its new price, the value is just undeniable.But I'd love to hear from you.Are you still using an iPhone 15?What has your experience been like?Or are you thinking of buying one?Let me know down in the comments.If you found this video helpful, a thumbs up would be amazing.And be sure to subscribe for more long-term reviews like this one.Thanks for watching and I'll catch you in the next

Whats going on guys, my name is Wade with TechDaily and in this video we are checking out the brand new iPhone 15.Now Apple actually changed a lot on the iPhone this year - it’s the first significant upgrade since maybe the iPhone 12 - and if you skipped the last few iPhones and were waiting for something decisively different, well I think that’s exactly what we have here with this new iPhone 15.So first things first, let’s quickly unbox it because there’s already something major inside the package we need to talk about.Ripping off those couple of stickers and sliding off the lid, the first thing you get is the phone itself of course.Obviously mine is in the Pink color.Next up is the usual “Designed by Apple in California” miniature packet of stuff, there’s a small instruction booklet and legal info along with one single Apple sticker.And the only other thing in the box is actually something totally new and different: a braided nylon USB-C to USB-C cable.Yes, the iPhone 15 this year ditches the lightning port and is now USB-C for charging and connecting accessories.This new cable that Apple includes is actually super nice too, its similar to the MacBook USB C cables, its thick, its high quality and you’re going to need it since all your old lightning cables will no longer work with this new iPhone.With all that stuff out of the way, here is the new iPhone 15 once again.Like I said, I opted for the Pink, it also comes in Black, Blue, Green and Yellow this year.The color options are maybe a little dull compared to years past, they’re a light pastel shade but still quite fun.And as far as pricing, the regular iPhone 15 once again starts at $799 for the 128GB model if you activate with a carrier right away, or $829 if you opt for the factory unlocked carrier-free option.Both the regular iPhone 15 and the larger iPhone 15 Plus actually are priced exactly the same with the same storage configurations options as last year, so any rumors of a price increase for these phones at least were untrue.Now at first glance, the new iPhone 15 actually more closely resembles last year’s iPhone 14 Pro both from the front and the back.It’s the same 6.1 inch size and same general form factor as like the last half dozen iPhones at this point, so that’s all unchanged.What is new is the Dynamic Island at the top there, this was a feature on last year’s iPhone 14 Pros and Apple brought it to the regular iPhone 15’s this year.If you’re unfamiliar, the Dynamic Island is this new take on the notch area or cutout that surrounds the selfie camera and FaceID sensors.It acts as this fluid information widget where different apps may display different things, so for example if you’re running a timer, it’ll display the countdown up there, if you’re listening to music or a podcast, you can quickly tap on the Dynamic Island to play, pause or scrub through your content.And it’s also where some of your notifications will be shown with a new animation and new way of presenting the information.There are a bunch of default Apple apps and 3rd party apps that utilize the dynamic island, and I actually think with this feature coming to the regular iPhone 15s now, we’ll hopefully see even more apps utilizing it.Around back, the new iPhone 15 now rocks a frosted glass finish - exactly like last year’s iPhone 14 Pro - and I think, beyond just being better at combating fingerprints, that this frosted glass finish makes the phone look and feel even more premium.One other minor design change: the color-matched aluminum edges on the new iPhone 15 are now rounded off ever so slightly, and aside from probably forcing you to buy a new case, this change also makes the phone feel a little slimmer and a little more comfortable in the hand.It’s still a relatively flat phone, but the sharper, harsh edges have been shaved down quite a bit.Aside from those minor physical changes, the iPhone 15 isn’t really any tougher or more durable this year.It’s covered in the same Ceramic Shield glass as last year with the same IP68 dust and water resistance rating.Taking a look around, on the left side this regular iPhone 15 retains the standard silent ringer switch, and I mention that only because the new iPhone 15 Pro’s have the action button instead, but thats not on the regular iPhone 15’s.There’s also the same volume buttons below that.On the right side, the familiar power button there just above a 5G antenna and of course no SIM card tray, U.S.IPhones are eSIM only again this year.At the bottom, a couple microphone and speaker cutouts flanking the new USB C charge port.Apple even color matched the little connector bit inside which is interesting, but obviously out of everything that’s new this year on the iPhone 15, I think the move to USB C is absolutely the most significant and maybe controversial or inconvenient change.On the one hand, it was bound to happen eventually.The iPads and MacBooks have been USB C for a while, and Apple may have also been pressured by the EU.But this is now the universal plug type for really all smartphones.So you can use your friend’s Samsung charging cable if you want, our you can loan them your iPhone cable now.It’s USB C for pretty much everyone.And while there’s definitely going to be a transitional period, where some people still may need a lightning cable or some people may not have enough USB C cables in their house, their car, at work, or wherever, eventually - like the change to lightning - this change to USB C will ultimately be the norm.And if you find yourself making the change to USB C with this new iPhone 15, just know that generally speaking, any USB C cables you find anywhere should be perfectly fine to charge this new iPhone.When it comes to the display, while Apple did upgrade the viewing experience slightly on the iPhone 15, it wasn’t really in the way some of us had hoped.The basic specs for this screen are more or less the same as last year: it’s a 6.1 inch Super Retina XDR OLED panel with HDR10 and Dolby Vision support.It comes in at a quirky 2556 by 1179 resolution for some 461 pixels per inch which is ever so slightly more pixels on screen than last year, due in part to the new dynamic island area which shrinks the “notch” and adds a tiny bit of screen real estate and also some people say the iPhone 15’s have smaller black bezels surrounding the display too, but I don’t see much of a difference at all there.What is different though is the max brightness: the iPhone 15’s display now peaks at 2000 nits via auto-brightness in outdoor viewing which is as bright as the iPhone 14 Pro’s display was last year, with the user controlled brightness peaking at about 1000 nits.That I think is a pretty worthwhile change and especially in certain sunny places -like I where I live in Las Vegas - the iPhone never seemed to be quite bright enough to see outside at times, so the brighter the screen the better.However, that’s the only real change with the screen.We don’t get the LTPO panel from the iPhone Pro’s, we don’t get the ProMotion 120hz, its still just a 60hz screen on this iPhone 15, we don’t get the always-on display option, so in that regard, this is still quite a few steps below the Pro iPhone’s again this year, but at least there’s something that’s been upgraded here.Inside, the new iPhone 15 is powered by Apple’s A16 Bionic chip paired to 6GB of RAM, which is the same combination of specs as last year’s iPhone 14 Pros.Remember, last year’s regular iPhone 14’s didn’t even get a processor upgrade at all, so with the new iPhone 15, we’re at least seeing a spec & performance boost over last year, even if its technically based on a one year old chipset.It’s also not as good as what’s in the new iPhone 15 Pro’s, so Apple kept that distinction once again, the Pro’s are more powerful, but all in all, at least we can see this year that new regular iPhone 15s have better specs.And of course, all the latest iOS 17 features and add-ons that certainly make the phone feel fresh, fast and new.Though iOS 17 is available on pretty much any iPhone from the last 5 or 6 years.As far as the battery life and charging speeds, surprisingly Apple didn’t really mention any better battery life or boost in juice with the iPhone 15’s this year.This phone has just a tiny bit larger battery size, 3349mah vs 3279 in the iPhone 14.But both the wired and wireless charging speeds are the same - even with the pivot to USB C.And there’s very little mention of better battery life or a longer lasting iPhone anywhere from Apple.What you will see though under the Battery Settings now is the ability to set an 80% charging limit to help reduce battery aging and better optimize charging.That’s probable due to a lot of us - me included - complaining about the bad battery health from the iPhone 14’s, and I’m interested to see if maybe Apple did some things there to at least help improve the battery health as a whole.Finally, as has been the case the last couple of years, Apple upgraded the iPhone 15’s camera setup quite a bit, and even brought over a few elements from last year’s iPhone 14 Pro camera setup.The dual lenses around back consist of a new 48mp main lens with some new capabilities, and seemingly the same 12mp ultrawide shooter.The selfie camera also appears to be about the same as last year, but inside the camera the iPhone 15 does have a few new tricks & features.For starters, we get 2x telephoto for better quality zoom shots and better portrait pics.There’s also additional format settings for shooting 12 or 24mp pictures and an additional option for 48mp pictures in standard 1x zoom daytime shooting conditions.And when it comes to your everyday photos, the actual results are going to look quite a bit better - particularly your portrait pictures along with low light and night time shots.Now I’m of course going to have a bunch of camera comparison test on the channel these new few weeks, but I’m really interested to see if maybe the camera setup on this new iPhone 15 is finally good enough to where there’s even less of a reason to go Pro - I suspect that might actually be the case.So what all is new with the new iPhone 15?Well, the dynamic island is new to this phone at least, the screen is brighter, the main camera is better, we actually get a processor and performance boost this year, the build of the phone I think is better too, and oh, USB-C is here for better or for worse.Collectively, there’s a lot that’s new with the iPhone 15, moreso than the last couple of iPhones for sure.If you were waiting to upgrade, if you skipped the iPhone 13 or iPhone 14, I really do think this new iPhone 15 may be “worth it” to upgrade to.But what do you guys think?Will you be getting the new iPhone 15?Let me know in the comments down below, I’d love to know your thoughts of course.Hopefully you guys did enjoy this video though.Be sure to follow TechDaily on Twitter and subscribe to the TechDaily YouTube channel if you haven’t already and I’ll see you guys later.

description.Now, let's move on to a big component of this screen, the dynamic island.The dynamic island itself is essentially a black bar that iOS uses to allow you to multitask.Currently, it can showcase your current song playing, your multiple timers that you may have, directions, and so many other various things.With the island out of the way, let's talk price.You can still find the iPhone 14 Pro Max from anywhere from $700 all the way to $1,100.So, let's talk about performance.When the iPhone 14 Pro Max first released, there were some performance related issues, but that was mainly due to the buggy mess that Apple called iOS 16.And most, if not all of those issues that plagued the iPhone 14 Pro Max during its initial launch have been resolved.And as one would expect from a flagship device, the iPhone 14 Pro Max is so fast.And the prootion display makes it feel even faster.Apps open so quickly.The camera opens fast.You can quickly record video.It is insane.I have had personally little to no slowdowns on this phone since I've gotten it.And in my opinion, this phone is an absolute powerhouse.One of the biggest things to me personally is battery life.If I'm spending $1,000 on an iPhone, I want to make sure that this thing lasts at least a day or two.And since launch, this thing has done just that.I use the phone moderately.I'm not constantly recording video or constantly streaming video on my phone.So, by the end of the night, my phone is usually at 50% battery life.And that is with the always on display turned on.The iPhone 14 Pro Max actually had the largest iPhone battery ever until the 15 Pro Max was released.The iPhone 14 Pro Max's battery is 4,323 MIA batteries, or as Apple says, up to 29 hours of video playback.And after over a year of use, we can see here that my battery health is at 92%.Speaking of life, let's talk about iOS upgradability.According to Apple's website, the most recent iOS update is available for all iPhones from the iPhone 10S all the way to the current flagship device.So, if you're going to be keeping the iPhone 14 Pro Max for 2 to 3 years, like most people do, you should have no worries getting iOS updates.Finally, I want to discuss something that is incredibly important.The cameras.The iPhone 14 Pro Max's cameras are absolutely beautiful.The biggest change is the 48 megapixel main camera.This is the first time since the iPhone 6S that Apple has changed the megapixel count in their main camera.To give you an idea, the iPhone 6S was released way back in 2015.The new 48 megapixel camera can also take raw photos.And wow, they are beautiful when you edit them up.Take a look at this little montage of photos and videos that I took directly out of the iPhone 14 Pro Max.Enjoy.[Music] So, what do you think?Is the iPhone 14 Pro Max's cameras good or are they pretty bad?Let me know down in the comment section below.I hope that montage gave you a better understanding of what the iPhone 14 Pro Max's cameras are capable of.Back to the $1,000 question.Is the iPhone 14 Pro Max worth it?Yes, but there's a few things you need to know.First, if you have an iPhone 13 Pro Max, you probably don't need this phone.Second, this is the first iPhone with a bunch of different new features.Considering Apple releases a brand new iPhone every single year, you're going to see a lot of features that the iPhone 14 Pro Max has as the first gen in those new iPhones.But if you have anything older than an iPhone 11 Pro Max, this is a huge upgrade.So, I hope this video gave you a better understanding of the iPhone 14 Pro Max.And thank you all so much for watching.I hope you enjoyed.Leave a thumbs up down below.Subscribe with those bell notifications turned on so you don't miss a single video.And your boy Anthony will see you next time.Peace.[Music]

The iphone 14s are official and this is my brutally honest take on them the good the bad and the ugly so let's start with the good the way that apple's managed to swap out the notch on past iphones with this much sleeker symmetrical hole punch it is legitimately impressive because it's not just an aesthetic improvement with this tiny little sliver of extra display you now unlock somehow making the phone look about three years more current but then it's also how they've managed to trim it like this while keeping the benefits of face id and then also turning it into a feature that they're calling dynamic island when this first came on i thought what are these animations that's such a gimmick but then realizing that this effectively allows you to multitask to see and change your music or check the progress of a timer you've set without leaving the app that you're already in i was very quickly all on board with it but then on top of that they've also managed to upgrade the selfie camera that goes inside of this hole punch and i think this is going to be a big hit we're in the tick tock generation there are millions of people almost exclusively using their front-facing cameras every single day to record and share themselves with the world and as someone who knows that most phone companies spend about 95 of their total camera budget on the rear cameras this pains me so i am extremely excited by the prospect of a front-facing camera that can let in more light create more separation between foreground and background and then use real autofocus to make sure that your face stays as that foreground and this will most likely extend the leads that apple already has when it comes to how snapchats and instagram stories are going to come across and then again on the almost surprising note of looking out for the customer the iphone 14s now also support direct to satellite communications which is it's considered the holy grail of connectivity it means that even if you're out of range of the traditional cell towers you can still send messages directly by the satellites orbiting the planets the only caveat being that the signal from them is going to be pretty weak by the time it reaches earth so you can only do things that require small amounts of data so for now apple's limited it to purely emergency services and keeping your family updated on your location if you're out and about remotely you can't use satellite to continue your search for love with tinder on the go as much as an emergency as that may feel like it is but still this feature is ahead of the curve the only slight alarm bell is the fact that this is a subscription service free for the first two years but then not after that there's a pretty big display upgrade on the new pro iphones slimmer screen borders although only slightly i'm actually finding it hard to tell at a glance and then a new brightness level of up to 2 000 nits which is nuts and also the highest brightness ever recorded on a smartphone okay well a lot of the most exciting things this time around only apply to the 14 pro and the pro max there is one big shake up to the non-pro iphones with the iphone 14s apple has swapped out the iphone mini that used to be the cheapest entry-level iphone for the iphone 14 plus which now sits above the base iphone 14 but below the pros probably for two reasons a the the mini was not a huge sales success like even though it was 25 of the iphone 13 lineup it only sold about five percent of the volume which i think is mostly due to the fact that smaller screens make the average person think that a phone is worthless but also b the simple fact that this has allowed apple to swap out the 699 iphone for what's going to be an 899 iphone and yes they give you a bigger screen and a bigger battery for that but that won't cost them close to the extra 200 that they're charging for it just leading to more profit per unit than before but that doesn't mean it's a bad option the main reason that i always go for these ultra pro max phones is because they're the only ones that can satisfy my battery life needs and also the fact that when you have a little bit of extra battery life headroom it prolongs how many years you can keep using it before the battery becomes an issue and so the fact that this iphone 14 plus can give you maybe three or four more hours in the standard iphone 14 without having to spring to the pro max makes it a viable option now the biggest upgrade that we had with last year's iphone 13 pro was something that apple calls the pro motion display a fancy way of saying a refresh rate that can scale all the way up to 120 hertz which makes the phone feel very responsive and smooth and all the way down to just 10 hertz which saves battery when you're looking at static pages well this year that 10 hertz lower limit has just become one hertz and that actually changes how you can use your phone it means much more potential battery saving but more importantly it's enabled apple to create a really efficient always on display the fact that the screen can refresh just one time per second paired with the oled panel it uses which allows the black pixels to turn off completely means that you can have a screen like this on all the time that can still keep you updated with any stat or calendar or notification with practically zero extra cost to battery life like with most things here not a feature that we've never seen before i used to have an amazon display on my samsung galaxy s8 like five years ago and through third-party apps you could make that always on display show almost anything you wanted it to but the value of what apple's offering here is a strong set of options that are visually consistent with the rest of the operating system easy to use with an even lower hit to your battery and without needing to go down the route of downloading apps that you don't particularly trust don't get me wrong there are absolutely things that apple does that irritate me to no end but i don't think the fact that they wait till they can do things really well is a negative what i do with my videos i'm never the first one to upload a video on a topic but i just like to think that that when i do post it's because i have a lot to say and so a sub to the channel would be applicable plus the way that this always-on display is integrated into the rest of the software is classy with most androids first your screen turns off then you're always on display just pops in it's it's like you're opening a separate piece of software but the iphone like some xiaomi phones with their super wallpapers transition subtly from your home screen to your always on display it makes it feel like a thoughtful feature like it's not just been tacked on at the end and i also imagine that once they add more features to this always display this will be the new lock screen and you'll just go straight from it to your home screen but we're not quite there yet and then just before things start to get worse they get better the rear cameras are also getting upgraded on the normal 14 and 14 plus that means a slightly better sensor which has better stabilization and a faster shutter time but then the more significant upgrade actually comes from the software apple's saying that this new image processing that they're calling the photonic engine can bring out two times more light when taking night shots that is a very exciting number but i just want to temper your expectations on that one because i've seen lots of big quotes like this over the years without much of a follow-through they also introduced action modes which is a super stabilized version of the normal camera good to have but android's have had this for years now and i never really use it because it completely kills the quality maybe apple's done it better though but then they went further with the pros for someone whose primary phone is an iphone and whose favorite part of androids is how exciting their camera setups are imagine my face when apple revealed that finally finally the iphone 14 pros are gonna come with a 48 megapixel main camera now 48 megapixel sensors aren't anything new to smartphones you can literally go to amazon and buy like a 200 phone that has one but the reason that i'm optimistic about this is that iphones have had 12 megapixel cameras for seven years now you've had companies left right and center just throwing out 108 megapixel sensors but apple has just stood there like nope 12 megapixel and this sends a very clear signal that apple isn't going to make their cameras higher resolution just to be able to say that they're high resolution they're only going to do it when they think it's enough of an upgrade for an average user to be able to tell so when you combine this new 48 megapixel camera with the fact that you can now take cinematic video in full 4k resolution that is huge for me by the way with the new ultrawide camera that the pro iphones also get the improved macro photography that comes as a part of that package with the new photonic engine that all of these iphone 14s are getting this is a camera system that you can bet i'm going to be fully testing on this channel and it's all topped off with one of the smartest camera features that i've seen in a long time an adaptive flash being able to recognize how far away your subject is and then adjusting the intensity of the flash module accordingly phones have been able to detect how far away your subject is for years now i'm actually shocked that it's taken this long for this feature to exist but it's a very good one there's also a new chip this time around the a16 bionic this is where things start to get a little hairy the angle apple took was to say well hey our a13 bionic chip from like three years ago is already more powerful than the competition we're only going to compare this new a16 to that chip which is pretty much a guarantee that if you compared it to last year's a15 it is not a big jump in terms of actual peak performance i appreciate the new 5g modem that reduces power consumption when you're using that 5g and also supports more 5g bands for better coverage which is actually much needed because right now my iphone is getting handily beaten by the competition in 5g capability but where it started to go properly downhill was when we realized that you're only getting this upgrade if you go for an iphone 14 pro we already saw this happening last year with the iphone 13s where the normal iphone 13s chip even though it had the exact same name was actually weaker than the iphone 13 pro's chip but this year apple's taken a step further by literally giving you one for one the same chip as last year with the same name as last year and listen to this now we are bringing this proven pro level performance to iphone 14.It is absolutely amazing that they are labeling this as if it is a feature and there's two ways of looking at this i've seen so many people just like who cares the iphone 13 is already so far ahead of androids with its chipsets in terms of both performance and performance per watt that nobody is going to notice the difference plus i guess you could also argue that this helps apple to get around the current chip shortage whilst also getting some extra use from chips they've already made that might otherwise have to be thrown away but on the other hand the way that i can't help but see it myself is that a phone's chipset is about so much more than performance the chip is responsible for how well your phone can take photos how efficient it is with battery how advanced its ai is there's not a lot that it isn't responsible for and so by getting last year's chip you're not just missing out on that extra 10 gaming performance you're effectively paying full 2022 pricing for a phone that's just generally bottlenecked by having last year's brain you certainly do not get 800 android flagships pulling this move which brings us to the not so good stuff about these phones like the design the iphone 14s do not look like badly designed phones but they are just incredibly uninspired the same sharp sides and corners the same materials an almost identical looking camera system and very similar color selection apart from that purple that's that's hard and this is intentional you can tell that apple is very confident with the way that their phones are designed right now and they aren't in the business of unnecessarily changing just for the sake of having something completely different which you can see through the designs are the past few phones literally the only thing that's changed is the size of the camera module and so my point is that in the face of other companies who are making much more significant design refinements as well as the rumors of what the iphone 14 could have been what we've actually gotten is not exactly setting the world on fire and then we get to the ugly almost definitely the least enjoyable part of this event was when we realized that all the best new features are being made exclusive to people buying the 14 pros and this is where it really clicked for me that apple is starting to create a noticeable divide in its user base when this company first started releasing upgraded versions of their existing phones it was a very simple case of hey this one's just bigger than the other one but at this point it's like the pro and the non-pro phones are a generation apart i can see why apple's doing it they're at a stage now where their existing standard phones are good enough for most people and so there's an increasing majority who who won't just drop everything to buy the top end model as soon as it's announced but will instead just wait until their current phone stops working and then upgrade to the base version of whatever happens to be the latest generation of iphones at the time and this is the bit that i don't love for all of these users who are just upgrading without really putting too much thought into it apple is trying to make sure that they're not giving them too many good features they're effectively selling them old attack at full price because these people may not notice the difference and then they're only actually giving the modern good stuff to the people who are both aware that they want it and are willing to pay more to get it to check out my unboxing of the tesla electrophone that video's here and i'll catch you there
